1	package try
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os/exec"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// NoChangesContract — TD-031 §8.1. The try package owns parsing and
12	// verification for no_changes attempts and returns a structured outcome to
13	// the execute loop. The loop only applies the decision it receives.
14	type NoChangesRationaleKind string
15	
16	const (
17		// NoChangesKindVerified means the rationale included a verification_command.
18		NoChangesKindVerified NoChangesRationaleKind = "verified"
19		// NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus means the rationale requested a canonical lifecycle status.
20		NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus NoChangesRationaleKind = "lifecycle_status"
21		// NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus means the rationale used a removed pseudo-status.
22		NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus NoChangesRationaleKind = "rejected_legacy_status"
23		// NoChangesKindUnjustified means neither structured marker was present.
24		NoChangesKindUnjustified NoChangesRationaleKind = "unjustified"
25	)
26	
27	const (
28		NoChangesEventVerified             = "no_changes_verified"
29		NoChangesEventUnverified           = "no_changes_unverified"
30		NoChangesEventUnjustified          = "no_changes_unjustified"
31		NoChangesEventAutonomousRetry      = "no_changes_autonomous_retry"
32		NoChangesEventOperatorRequired     = "no_changes_operator_required"
33		NoChangesEventBlocked              = "no_changes_blocked"
34		NoChangesEventLegacyStatusRejected = "no_changes_legacy_status_rejected"
35	)
36	
37	const (
38		NoChangesLabelUnverified  = "triage:no-changes-unverified"
39		NoChangesLabelUnjustified = "triage:no-changes-unjustified"
40	)
41	
42	type ParsedNoChangesRationale struct {
43		Kind                NoChangesRationaleKind
44		VerificationCommand string
45		LifecycleStatus     string
46		Reason              string
47		SuggestedAction     string
48		RejectionReason     string
49	}
50	
51	func ParseNoChangesRationale(text string) ParsedNoChangesRationale {
52		p := ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified}
53		var reasonLines []string
54		inReason := false
55		for _, raw := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
56			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
57			lower := strings.ToLower(line)
58			switch {
59			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "verification_command:"):
60				cmd := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("verification_command:"):])
61				if cmd != "" {
62					p.VerificationCommand = cmd
63					p.Kind = NoChangesKindVerified
64				}
65				inReason = false
66			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "status:"):
67				v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(line[len("status:"):]))
68				p.LifecycleStatus = v
69				inReason = false
70			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "reason:"):
71				r := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("reason:"):])
72				if r != "" {
73					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, r)
74				}
75				inReason = true
76			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "suggested_action:"):
77				p.SuggestedAction = strings.TrimSpace(line[len("suggested_action:"):])
78				inReason = false
79			default:
80				if inReason && line != "" {
81					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, line)
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		if p.Kind == NoChangesKindVerified {
86			p.LifecycleStatus = ""
87			p.Reason = ""
88			p.SuggestedAction = ""
89			p.RejectionReason = ""
90			return p
91		}
92		p.Reason = strings.Join(reasonLines, " ")
93		switch p.LifecycleStatus {
94		case "":
95			return p
96		case "needs_investigation":
97			p.Kind = NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus
98			p.RejectionReason = "status: needs_investigation is no longer accepted; use status: open, status: proposed, or status: blocked for new no_changes output, and run `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle` for stored legacy rows"
99		case "open", "proposed", "blocked", "closed", "cancelled":
100			p.Kind = NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus
101		default:
102			p.Kind = NoChangesKindUnjustified
103		}
104		return p
105	}
106	
107	type VerificationCommandRunner func(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (exitCode int, output string, err error)
108	
109	const DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second
110	
111	func DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (int, string, error) {
112		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
113		defer cancel()
114		cmd := exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)
115		if projectRoot != "" {
116			cmd.Dir = projectRoot
117		}
118		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
119		if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
120			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), fmt.Errorf("verification_command timed out after %s", DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
121		}
122		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
123			return exitErr.ExitCode(), truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
124		}
125		if err != nil {
126			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), err
127		}
128		return 0, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
129	}
130	
131	func truncateVerifyOutput(s string) string {
132		const max = 4 * 1024
133		if len(s) <= max {
134			return s
135		}
136		return s[:max] + "\n...[truncated]"
137	}
138	
139	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
140		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
141	}
142	
143	type NoChangesOutcome struct {
144		Satisfied        bool
145		Action           NoChangesLifecycleAction
146		CooldownEligible bool
147		Evidence         string
148		EventKind        string
149		EventBody        string
150		Label            string
151		LifecycleStatus  string
152		Reason           string
153		SuggestedAction  string
154	}
155	
156	type NoChangesLifecycleAction string
157	
158	const (
159		NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied NoChangesLifecycleAction = "close_already_satisfied"
160		NoChangesActionKeepOpenSmartRetry    NoChangesLifecycleAction = "keep_open_smart_retry"
161		NoChangesActionOperatorRequired      NoChangesLifecycleAction = "operator_required"
162		NoChangesActionBlockedExternal       NoChangesLifecycleAction = "blocked_external"
163		NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown  NoChangesLifecycleAction = "bad_attempt_no_cooldown"
164		NoChangesActionRetryLaterCooldown    NoChangesLifecycleAction = "retry_later_cooldown"
165	)
166	
167	func adjudicateNoChangesContract(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report Report, projectRoot string, noChangesCount int, checker SatisfactionChecker, runner VerificationCommandRunner) (NoChangesOutcome, Report, error) {
168		if checker != nil {
169			satisfied, evidence, err := checker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
170			if err != nil {
171				return NoChangesOutcome{}, report, err
172			}
173			if satisfied {
174				if evidence != "" {
175					report.Detail = evidence
176				}
177				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
178				return NoChangesOutcome{
179					Satisfied:        true,
180					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
181					CooldownEligible: false,
182					Evidence:         evidence,
183				}, report, nil
184			}
185			return NoChangesOutcome{
186				Satisfied:        false,
187				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
188				CooldownEligible: false,
189			}, report, nil
190		}
191	
192		parsed := ParseNoChangesRationale(report.NoChangesRationale)
193		switch parsed.Kind {
194		case NoChangesKindVerified:
195			if runner == nil {
196				runner = DefaultVerificationCommandRunner
197			}
198			exitCode, output, runErr := runner(ctx, projectRoot, parsed.VerificationCommand)
199			body := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command=%s\nexit_code=%d", parsed.VerificationCommand, exitCode)
200			if output != "" {
201				body += "\noutput:\n" + output
202			}
203			if runErr != nil {
204				body += "\nrun_error: " + runErr.Error()
205			}
206			if runErr == nil && exitCode == 0 {
207				evidence := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command passed: %s", parsed.VerificationCommand)
208				report.Detail = evidence
209				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
210				return NoChangesOutcome{
211					Satisfied:        true,
212					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
213					CooldownEligible: false,
214					Evidence:         evidence,
215					EventKind:        NoChangesEventVerified,
216					EventBody:        body,
217				}, report, nil
218			}
219			return NoChangesOutcome{
220				Satisfied:        false,
221				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
222				CooldownEligible: false,
223				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnverified,
224				EventBody:        body,
225				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnverified,
226			}, report, nil
227		case NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus:
228			return adjudicateNoChangesLifecycleStatus(parsed, report), report, nil
229		case NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus:
230			body := parsed.RejectionReason
231			if parsed.Reason != "" {
232				body += "\nreason: " + parsed.Reason
233			}
234			return NoChangesOutcome{
235				Satisfied:        false,
236				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
237				CooldownEligible: false,
238				EventKind:        NoChangesEventLegacyStatusRejected,
239				EventBody:        body,
240				Reason:           body,
241			}, report, nil
242		default:
243			body := strings.TrimSpace(report.NoChangesRationale)
244			if body == "" {
245				body = "(rationale absent)"
246			}
247			return NoChangesOutcome{
248				Satisfied:        false,
249				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
250				CooldownEligible: false,
251				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
252				EventBody:        body,
253				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnjustified,
254			}, report, nil
255		}
256	}
257	
258	func adjudicateNoChangesLifecycleStatus(parsed ParsedNoChangesRationale, report Report) NoChangesOutcome {
259		reason := strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Reason)
260		suggestedAction := strings.TrimSpace(parsed.SuggestedAction)
261		switch parsed.LifecycleStatus {
262		case "open":
263			if reason == "" {
264				reason = "autonomous work remains possible"
265			}
266			if suggestedAction == "" {
267				suggestedAction = "retry with a smart agent"
268			}
269			return NoChangesOutcome{
270				Satisfied:        false,
271				Action:           NoChangesActionKeepOpenSmartRetry,
272				CooldownEligible: false,
273				EventKind:        NoChangesEventAutonomousRetry,
274				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
275				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
276				Reason:           reason,
277				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
278			}
279		case "proposed":
280			if reason == "" {
281				reason = "operator decision required before another automated attempt"
282			}
283			if suggestedAction == "" {
284				suggestedAction = "review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work"
285			}
286			return NoChangesOutcome{
287				Satisfied:        false,
288				Action:           NoChangesActionOperatorRequired,
289				CooldownEligible: false,
290				EventKind:        NoChangesEventOperatorRequired,
291				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
292				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
293				Reason:           reason,
294				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
295			}
296		case "blocked":
297			if reason == "" {
298				return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "status: blocked requires reason: <external recheckable blocker>")
299			}
300			if suggestedAction == "" {
301				suggestedAction = "recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared"
302			}
303			return NoChangesOutcome{
304				Satisfied:        false,
305				Action:           NoChangesActionBlockedExternal,
306				CooldownEligible: false,
307				EventKind:        NoChangesEventBlocked,
308				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
309				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
310				Reason:           reason,
311				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
312			}
313		case "closed":
314			return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "use verification_command to close an already-satisfied bead")
315		case "cancelled":
316			return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "cancelling work is an operator action, not a no_changes worker outcome")
317		default:
318			body := strings.TrimSpace(report.NoChangesRationale)
319			if body == "" {
320				body = "(rationale absent)"
321			}
322			return NoChangesOutcome{
323				Satisfied:        false,
324				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
325				CooldownEligible: false,
326				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
327				EventBody:        body,
328				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnjustified,
329			}
330		}
331	}
332	
333	func noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(status, reason string) NoChangesOutcome {
334		body := fmt.Sprintf("status: %s rejected: %s", status, reason)
335		return NoChangesOutcome{
336			Satisfied:        false,
337			Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
338			CooldownEligible: false,
339			EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
340			EventBody:        body,
341			Reason:           body,
342		}
343	}
344	
345	func noChangesLifecycleEventBody(status, reason, suggestedAction string) string {
346		body := "status=" + status
347		if reason != "" {
348			body += "\nreason=" + reason
349		}
350		if suggestedAction != "" {
351			body += "\nsuggested_action=" + suggestedAction
352		}
353		return body
354	}

1	# Intake Gate — Pre-Claim Actionability And Complexity Evaluator
2	
3	The intake gate is a pre-Claim phase in `ddx work` that evaluates each
4	candidate bead before an agent worker claims it. Its purpose is to improve beads
5	that can be safely clarified, prevent coarse epics from being dispatched as
6	monolithic work items, and hold ambiguous work for operator decision before an
7	implementer is forced to guess.
8	
9	## When the gate triggers
10	
11	The gate runs for every ready bead before `Store.Claim`. It evaluates the bead's
12	title, description, acceptance criteria, labels, parent, dependencies, spec-id,
13	and prior attempt history to classify it as:
14	
15	- **actionable_atomic** — a single coherent unit of work; proceeds to Claim
16	  normally.
17	- **actionable_but_rewritten** — the gate made a validated replacement rewrite
18	  or other safe, intent-preserving bead update through `ddx bead update`;
19	  proceeds to Claim after the mutation.
20	- **too_large_decomposed** — multiple independent deliverables; the gate files
21	  child beads, records the AC map, and leaves the parent `status=open` with
22	  dependency edges to the children.
23	- **ambiguous_requires_operator** — needs human clarification; the gate moves
24	  the bead to `status=proposed`.
25	
26	Safe rewrites may replace the bead body when the replacement is clearer,
27	execution-ready, and validated against durable anchors. The gate should not
28	append clarifying noise simply to satisfy preservation: the output bead is the
29	next agent's prompt, so prompt fitness is part of correctness. Some replacements
30	expand a vague one-line bead into a standalone task; others compress noisy or
31	stale prose. The original text is retained in readiness evidence with
32	before/after hashes.
33	
34	Validated preservation is based on explicit commitments and durable context:
35	acceptance criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies,
36	named files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence. The gate may replace
37	stale line numbers or chat-shaped prose with current section anchors or file:line
38	evidence. It must not invent product behavior, change scope, choose between
39	conflicting requirements, delete unresolved constraints, or guess a missing
40	governing artifact.
41	
42	The same decomposition policy is also used as a post-attempt safety net. If an
43	implementation attempt returns `no_changes_needs_investigation` or an equivalent
44	structured outcome whose rationale says the bead is too large or cannot be
45	safely split inside the implementation worktree, `ddx work` must run the
46	orchestrator-level splitter on the original bead. That fallback is not operator
47	work unless the splitter cannot produce a lossless AC map or the configured
48	decomposition depth has truly been exhausted at the queue level; those cases
49	move the bead to `status=proposed`.
50	
51	## Bypassing the gate per-bead
52	
53	Add the label `triage:skip` to a bead to bypass the complexity gate entirely:
54	
55	```
56	ddx bead update <id> --labels triage:skip
57	```
58	
59	The bead will proceed directly to Claim on the next dispatch cycle. Use this
60	for beads you have manually reviewed and confirmed are atomic, or for beads
61	that the gate misclassifies.
62	
63	## Decomposed parent state
64	
65	When the gate decomposes a bead:
66	
67	1. Child beads are filed with `parent: <id>` linking back to the epic.
68	2. The parent's status remains `open`; dependency waiting is derived from the
69	   new child dependency edges, not `status=blocked`.
70	3. The parent receives a `kind:triage-decomposed` event whose JSON body lists
71	   the `child_ids`, the splitter's `rationale`, and an `ac_map`.
72	4. Dependency edges are added: the parent depends on all children, ensuring it
73	   cannot be dispatched again until children close (should it be re-opened).
74	
75	The `ac_map` is load-bearing. Every parent acceptance criterion must map to at
76	least one child acceptance criterion or be explicitly marked
77	`operator_required` or `non_scope` with a rationale. A split that drops an AC is
78	invalid and moves the parent to `status=proposed` instead of dispatching
79	children as if the work were complete.
80	
81	Children re-enter the triage gate on their next dispatch cycle. If a child is
82	itself decomposable, the gate will split it further — up to the depth cap.
83	
84	## Recursion depth cap
85	
86	Default depth cap: **3** levels. Configurable in `.ddx/config.yaml` at
87	`agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`:
88	
89	```yaml
90	agent:
91	  triage:
92	    max_decomposition_depth: 3
93	```
94	
95	When a bead at the queue-level depth cap is evaluated, the gate:
96	
97	1. Appends a `kind:triage-overflow` event.
98	2. Sets `status=proposed` and records the depth overflow in triage evidence.
99	3. Does **not** invoke the classifier or splitter.
100	4. Does **not** dispatch the bead.
101	
102	Implementation-attempt depth is not the same as queue-level decomposition depth.
103	If a worker reports that it cannot split because the attempt prompt forbids
104	another child layer, the orchestrator still owns the next split decision unless
105	the bead itself has reached `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`.
106	
107	The operator must manually split the bead or add `triage:skip` to bypass only
108	after queue-level overflow or lossy/ambiguous decomposition is recorded.
109	
110	## AC-coverage metric
111	
112	The `bead-split` prompt is evaluated against a held-out corpus using both:
113	
114	- hard AC traceability: every parent AC maps to child ACs, `operator_required`, or
115	  `non_scope`;
116	- string-overlap metric: the fraction of unique AC tokens (alphanumeric tokens
117	  ≥ 3 chars) from the parent's acceptance criteria that appear in the combined
118	  acceptance criteria of the child specs.
119	
120	A rate ≥ 90% is required for the heuristic metric, but overlap alone is not
121	acceptance. The hard AC map is the gate that prevents lossy decomposition.
122	
123	## Regenerating the historical corpus
124	
125	The frozen eval slice lives in `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl`. To
126	regenerate from live bead history:
127	
128	```bash
129	go run ./scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go \
130	  --output library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl
131	```
132	
133	The harvest script reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` from this repository and includes
134	`../agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` when that checkout is present. It produces a
135	deterministic held-out eval slice. Running it with the same inputs always
136	produces the same corpus file.
137	
138	## Ownership boundary
139	
140	DDx owns orchestration, tracking, work sizing, child bead creation, parent
141	dependencies, and retry decisions. The agent owns provider/model/harness
142	routing and execution. The triage gate must not choose or rank concrete
143	providers, models, or harnesses; if an operator supplied those values, DDx
144	passes them through as opaque constraints to the agent runtime.
145	
146	Decomposition is a high-judgment routing decision. The splitter must run through
147	the normal `ddx work` path with Fizeau's `smart` model-ref and a strong
148	`MinPower` floor, defaulting to the smart/top-power tier floor when the project
149	has no explicit splitter override. DDx still does not pick a concrete model: it
150	only requests the abstract smart ref, raises the power floor, and lets the agent
151	route within any operator-supplied passthrough constraints. If no available
152	route satisfies that strong floor, DDx records readiness as unavailable
153	(`readiness_error` / `intake_error`) instead of marking the bead as ambiguous or
154	moving it to `status=proposed`. DDx must not silently fall back to weak
155	decomposition; the worker continues, skips, or stops according to the configured
156	readiness-failure mode.
157	
158	## Disabling the gate
159	
160	The gate is disabled when `ExecuteBeadWorker.ComplexityGate == nil`. This is
161	intentionally non-silent: the worker emits a one-time warning per boot:
162	
163	```
164	warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
165	```
166	
167	The gate should only be disabled in test fixtures or during development. In
168	production, always wire a `ComplexityGate` via `NewComplexityGate`.
169	
170	## Tracker events
171	
172	| Event kind              | When                                       | Body                          |
173	|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
174	| `triage-rewritten`      | Safe bead replacement/update applied       | `{fields, rationale, before, after}` |
175	| `triage-decomposed`     | Parent split into children                 | `{child_ids, rationale, ac_map}` |
176	| `triage-overflow`       | Bead at depth cap, blocked                 | `{depth, max}`                |
177	| `triage-ambiguous`      | Gate returned ambiguous classification     | `{confidence, reasoning}`     |
178	| `triage.gate_disabled`  | Loop boot with nil gate (loop event log)   | `{bead_id}`                   |
179	| `triage.skipped`        | Gate returned shouldClaim=false            | `{bead_id}`                   |
180	| `triage.error`          | Gate returned a non-nil error              | `{bead_id, reason}`           |
181

1789	//   - Step 0 size-check + decomposition (ddx bead create / dep add / update)
1790	//   - Address every BLOCKING <review-findings> item; never declare
1791	//     no_changes with blocking findings open
1792	//   - Stop after the commit (Agent post-commit runaway guard)
1793	//   - Agent variant only: use tool calls, not `bash: cat`/`rg`/`ls`
1794	
1795	// instrStep0SizeCheck is the shared Step 0 size-check + decomposition recipe.
1796	// Both variants emit it verbatim; per-variant preamble runs before it.
1797	const instrStep0SizeCheck = `
1798	
1799	## Step 0: size check
1800	
1801	The bead is too big when any of these holds:
1802	
1803	- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
1804	- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
1805	- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
1806	- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
1807	- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
1808	- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.
1809	
1810	If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:
1811	
1812	1. ` + "`ddx bead create`" + ` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
1813	2. ` + "`ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>`" + ` to record edges.
1814	3. ` + "`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`" + ` so the decomposition is visible.
1815	4. Write ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.
1816	
1817	A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.`
1818	
1819	// instrNoChangesContract is the shared NoChangesContract (TD-031 §8.1) rule.
1820	const instrNoChangesContract = `
1821	
1822	## no_changes contract
1823	
1824	` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/no_changes_rationale.txt`" + ` must contain one:
1825	
1826	- ` + "`verification_command: <cmd>`" + ` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
1827	- ` + "`status: open`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <retryable>`" + ` — open, smart retry.
1828	- ` + "`status: proposed`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <operator needed>`" + ` — operator lane.
1829	- ` + "`status: blocked`" + ` + ` + "`reason: <external blocker>`" + ` — blocked lane.
1830	
1831	No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and ` + "`status: needs_investigation`" + ` reject.`
1832	
1833	// instrInvestigationReports is the shared report-output rule.
1834	const instrInvestigationReports = `
1835	
1836	## Reports
1837	
1838	Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under ` + "`{{.AttemptDir}}/`" + ` (the per-attempt evidence directory under ` + "`.ddx/executions/`" + `). **Never write reports to ` + "`/tmp`" + ` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. 1	package bead

3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"

8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"
74	)
75	
76	// CanonicalStatuses is the single source of truth for the persisted bead
77	// status enumeration. It mirrors the bd/br canonical set documented in
78	// TD-031 §2 and the JSON Schema enum at
79	// cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json. The CI guard tests in
80	// sync_test.go assert that schema, TD doc, and Go source agree with this
81	// list; adding or removing a value here without updating the schema and TD
82	// will fail CI.
83	var CanonicalStatuses = []string{
84		StatusOpen,
85		StatusInProgress,
86		StatusClosed,
87		StatusBlocked,
88		StatusProposed,
89		StatusCancelled,
90	}
91	
92	// IsCanonicalStatus reports whether s is one of the persisted bead statuses
93	// in CanonicalStatuses.
94	func IsCanonicalStatus(s string) bool {
95		for _, c := range CanonicalStatuses {
96			if c == s {
97				return true
98			}
99		}
100		return false
101	}

103	// IssueType constants
104	const (
105		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)
109	
110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)
120	
121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}
145	
146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}
182	
183	// Default values
184	const (
185		DefaultType     = "task"
186		DefaultStatus   = StatusOpen
187		DefaultPriority = 2
188		DefaultPrefix   = "bx" // used only when repo name detection fails
189		MinPriority     = 0
190		MaxPriority     = 4
191	)
192	
193	// StatusCounts holds aggregate counts for a bead store.
194	type StatusCounts struct {
195		Open              int `json:"open"`
196		InProgress        int `json:"in_progress"`
197		Closed            int `json:"closed"`
198		Blocked           int `json:"blocked"`
199		Proposed          int `json:"proposed"`
200		Cancelled         int `json:"cancelled"`
201		Ready             int `json:"ready"`
202		NeedsHuman        int `json:"needs_human"`
203		WorkerReady       int `json:"worker_ready"`
204		DependencyWaiting int `json:"dependency_waiting"`
205		ExternalBlocked   int `json:"external_blocked"`
206		OperatorAttention int `json:"operator_attention"`
207		Total             int `json:"total"`
208	}
209	
210	// Blocker kinds surfaced through BlockedAll. These strings are part of the
211	// external DDx contract (HELIX reads them to decide how to handle a blocker).
212	const (
213		BlockerKindDependency         = "dependency"
214		BlockerKindBlockedStatus      = "blocked-status"
215		BlockerKindRetryCooldown      = "retry-cooldown"
216		BlockerKindNeedsInvestigation = "needs-investigation"
217		BlockerKindOperatorAttention  = "operator-attention"
218		BlockerKindNotEligible        = "not-execution-eligible"
219		BlockerKindSuperseded         = "superseded"
220		BlockerKindEpicOnly           = "epic-only"
221	)
222	
223	// Blocker describes why an open bead is currently not runnable. Either
224	// unclosed dependencies exist, or an execute-loop cooldown has parked the
225	// bead until NextEligibleAt.
226	type Blocker struct {
227		Kind           string   `json:"kind"`
228		NextEligibleAt string   `json:"next_eligible_at,omitempty"`
229		UnclosedDepIDs []string `json:"unclosed_dep_ids,omitempty"`
230		LastStatus     string   `json:"last_status,omitempty"`
231		LastDetail     string   `json:"last_detail,omitempty"`
232		Reason         string   `json:"reason,omitempty"`
233	}
234	
235	// BlockedBead pairs a bead with its blocker classification.
236	type BlockedBead struct {
237		Bead
238		Blocker Blocker `json:"blocker"`
239	}
240	
241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

1	package try
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os/exec"
7		"strings"
8		"time"
9	)
10	
11	// NoChangesContract — TD-031 §8.1. The try package owns parsing and
12	// verification for no_changes attempts and returns a structured outcome to
13	// the execute loop. The loop only applies the decision it receives.
14	type NoChangesRationaleKind string
15	
16	const (
17		// NoChangesKindVerified means the rationale included a verification_command.
18		NoChangesKindVerified NoChangesRationaleKind = "verified"
19		// NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus means the rationale requested a canonical lifecycle status.
20		NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus NoChangesRationaleKind = "lifecycle_status"
21		// NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus means the rationale used a removed pseudo-status.
22		NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus NoChangesRationaleKind = "rejected_legacy_status"
23		// NoChangesKindUnjustified means neither structured marker was present.
24		NoChangesKindUnjustified NoChangesRationaleKind = "unjustified"
25	)
26	
27	const (
28		NoChangesEventVerified             = "no_changes_verified"
29		NoChangesEventUnverified           = "no_changes_unverified"
30		NoChangesEventUnjustified          = "no_changes_unjustified"
31		NoChangesEventAutonomousRetry      = "no_changes_autonomous_retry"
32		NoChangesEventOperatorRequired     = "no_changes_operator_required"
33		NoChangesEventBlocked              = "no_changes_blocked"
34		NoChangesEventLegacyStatusRejected = "no_changes_legacy_status_rejected"
35	)
36	
37	const (
38		NoChangesLabelUnverified  = "triage:no-changes-unverified"
39		NoChangesLabelUnjustified = "triage:no-changes-unjustified"
40	)
41	
42	type ParsedNoChangesRationale struct {
43		Kind                NoChangesRationaleKind
44		VerificationCommand string
45		LifecycleStatus     string
46		Reason              string
47		SuggestedAction     string
48		RejectionReason     string
49	}
50	
51	func ParseNoChangesRationale(text string) ParsedNoChangesRationale {
52		p := ParsedNoChangesRationale{Kind: NoChangesKindUnjustified}
53		var reasonLines []string
54		inReason := false
55		for _, raw := range strings.Split(text, "\n") {
56			line := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
57			lower := strings.ToLower(line)
58			switch {
59			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "verification_command:"):
60				cmd := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("verification_command:"):])
61				if cmd != "" {
62					p.VerificationCommand = cmd
63					p.Kind = NoChangesKindVerified
64				}
65				inReason = false
66			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "status:"):
67				v := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(line[len("status:"):]))
68				p.LifecycleStatus = v
69				inReason = false
70			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "reason:"):
71				r := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("reason:"):])
72				if r != "" {
73					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, r)
74				}
75				inReason = true
76			case strings.HasPrefix(lower, "suggested_action:"):
77				p.SuggestedAction = strings.TrimSpace(line[len("suggested_action:"):])
78				inReason = false
79			default:
80				if inReason && line != "" {
81					reasonLines = append(reasonLines, line)
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		if p.Kind == NoChangesKindVerified {
86			p.LifecycleStatus = ""
87			p.Reason = ""
88			p.SuggestedAction = ""
89			p.RejectionReason = ""
90			return p
91		}
92		p.Reason = strings.Join(reasonLines, " ")
93		switch p.LifecycleStatus {
94		case "":
95			return p
96		case "needs_investigation":
97			p.Kind = NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus
98			p.RejectionReason = "status: needs_investigation is no longer accepted; use status: open, status: proposed, or status: blocked for new no_changes output, and run `ddx bead migrate --lifecycle` for stored legacy rows"
99		case "open", "proposed", "blocked", "closed", "cancelled":
100			p.Kind = NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus
101		default:
102			p.Kind = NoChangesKindUnjustified
103		}
104		return p
105	}
106	
107	type VerificationCommandRunner func(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (exitCode int, output string, err error)
108	
109	const DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout = 60 * time.Second
110	
111	func DefaultVerificationCommandRunner(ctx context.Context, projectRoot, command string) (int, string, error) {
112		cctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
113		defer cancel()
114		cmd := exec.CommandContext(cctx, "/bin/sh", "-c", command)
115		if projectRoot != "" {
116			cmd.Dir = projectRoot
117		}
118		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
119		if cctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
120			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), fmt.Errorf("verification_command timed out after %s", DefaultVerificationCommandTimeout)
121		}
122		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
123			return exitErr.ExitCode(), truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
124		}
125		if err != nil {
126			return -1, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), err
127		}
128		return 0, truncateVerifyOutput(string(out)), nil
129	}
130	
131	func truncateVerifyOutput(s string) string {
132		const max = 4 * 1024
133		if len(s) <= max {
134			return s
135		}
136		return s[:max] + "\n...[truncated]"
137	}
138	
139	type SatisfactionChecker interface {
140		CheckSatisfied(ctx context.Context, beadID string, noChangesCount int) (bool, string, error)
141	}
142	
143	type NoChangesOutcome struct {
144		Satisfied        bool
145		Action           NoChangesLifecycleAction
146		CooldownEligible bool
147		Evidence         string
148		EventKind        string
149		EventBody        string
150		Label            string
151		LifecycleStatus  string
152		Reason           string
153		SuggestedAction  string
154	}
155	
156	type NoChangesLifecycleAction string
157	
158	const (
159		NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied NoChangesLifecycleAction = "close_already_satisfied"
160		NoChangesActionKeepOpenSmartRetry    NoChangesLifecycleAction = "keep_open_smart_retry"
161		NoChangesActionOperatorRequired      NoChangesLifecycleAction = "operator_required"
162		NoChangesActionBlockedExternal       NoChangesLifecycleAction = "blocked_external"
163		NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown  NoChangesLifecycleAction = "bad_attempt_no_cooldown"
164		NoChangesActionRetryLaterCooldown    NoChangesLifecycleAction = "retry_later_cooldown"
165	)
166	
167	func adjudicateNoChangesContract(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report Report, projectRoot string, noChangesCount int, checker SatisfactionChecker, runner VerificationCommandRunner) (NoChangesOutcome, Report, error) {
168		if checker != nil {
169			satisfied, evidence, err := checker.CheckSatisfied(ctx, beadID, noChangesCount)
170			if err != nil {
171				return NoChangesOutcome{}, report, err
172			}
173			if satisfied {
174				if evidence != "" {
175					report.Detail = evidence
176				}
177				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
178				return NoChangesOutcome{
179					Satisfied:        true,
180					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
181					CooldownEligible: false,
182					Evidence:         evidence,
183				}, report, nil
184			}
185			return NoChangesOutcome{
186				Satisfied:        false,
187				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
188				CooldownEligible: false,
189			}, report, nil
190		}
191	
192		parsed := ParseNoChangesRationale(report.NoChangesRationale)
193		switch parsed.Kind {
194		case NoChangesKindVerified:
195			if runner == nil {
196				runner = DefaultVerificationCommandRunner
197			}
198			exitCode, output, runErr := runner(ctx, projectRoot, parsed.VerificationCommand)
199			body := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command=%s\nexit_code=%d", parsed.VerificationCommand, exitCode)
200			if output != "" {
201				body += "\noutput:\n" + output
202			}
203			if runErr != nil {
204				body += "\nrun_error: " + runErr.Error()
205			}
206			if runErr == nil && exitCode == 0 {
207				evidence := fmt.Sprintf("verification_command passed: %s", parsed.VerificationCommand)
208				report.Detail = evidence
209				report.Status = StatusAlreadySatisfied
210				return NoChangesOutcome{
211					Satisfied:        true,
212					Action:           NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied,
213					CooldownEligible: false,
214					Evidence:         evidence,
215					EventKind:        NoChangesEventVerified,
216					EventBody:        body,
217				}, report, nil
218			}
219			return NoChangesOutcome{
220				Satisfied:        false,
221				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
222				CooldownEligible: false,
223				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnverified,
224				EventBody:        body,
225				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnverified,
226			}, report, nil
227		case NoChangesKindLifecycleStatus:
228			return adjudicateNoChangesLifecycleStatus(parsed, report), report, nil
229		case NoChangesKindRejectedLegacyStatus:
230			body := parsed.RejectionReason
231			if parsed.Reason != "" {
232				body += "\nreason: " + parsed.Reason
233			}
234			return NoChangesOutcome{
235				Satisfied:        false,
236				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
237				CooldownEligible: false,
238				EventKind:        NoChangesEventLegacyStatusRejected,
239				EventBody:        body,
240				Reason:           body,
241			}, report, nil
242		default:
243			body := strings.TrimSpace(report.NoChangesRationale)
244			if body == "" {
245				body = "(rationale absent)"
246			}
247			return NoChangesOutcome{
248				Satisfied:        false,
249				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
250				CooldownEligible: false,
251				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
252				EventBody:        body,
253				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnjustified,
254			}, report, nil
255		}
256	}
257	
258	func adjudicateNoChangesLifecycleStatus(parsed ParsedNoChangesRationale, report Report) NoChangesOutcome {
259		reason := strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Reason)
260		suggestedAction := strings.TrimSpace(parsed.SuggestedAction)
261		switch parsed.LifecycleStatus {
262		case "open":
263			if reason == "" {
264				reason = "autonomous work remains possible"
265			}
266			if suggestedAction == "" {
267				suggestedAction = "retry with a smart agent"
268			}
269			return NoChangesOutcome{
270				Satisfied:        false,
271				Action:           NoChangesActionKeepOpenSmartRetry,
272				CooldownEligible: false,
273				EventKind:        NoChangesEventAutonomousRetry,
274				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
275				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
276				Reason:           reason,
277				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
278			}
279		case "proposed":
280			if reason == "" {
281				reason = "operator decision required before another automated attempt"
282			}
283			if suggestedAction == "" {
284				suggestedAction = "review and accept, split, block, or cancel this proposed work"
285			}
286			return NoChangesOutcome{
287				Satisfied:        false,
288				Action:           NoChangesActionOperatorRequired,
289				CooldownEligible: false,
290				EventKind:        NoChangesEventOperatorRequired,
291				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
292				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
293				Reason:           reason,
294				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
295			}
296		case "blocked":
297			if reason == "" {
298				return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "status: blocked requires reason: <external recheckable blocker>")
299			}
300			if suggestedAction == "" {
301				suggestedAction = "recheck the external blocker and move status to open when cleared"
302			}
303			return NoChangesOutcome{
304				Satisfied:        false,
305				Action:           NoChangesActionBlockedExternal,
306				CooldownEligible: false,
307				EventKind:        NoChangesEventBlocked,
308				EventBody:        noChangesLifecycleEventBody(parsed.LifecycleStatus, reason, suggestedAction),
309				LifecycleStatus:  parsed.LifecycleStatus,
310				Reason:           reason,
311				SuggestedAction:  suggestedAction,
312			}
313		case "closed":
314			return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "use verification_command to close an already-satisfied bead")
315		case "cancelled":
316			return noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(parsed.LifecycleStatus, "cancelling work is an operator action, not a no_changes worker outcome")
317		default:
318			body := strings.TrimSpace(report.NoChangesRationale)
319			if body == "" {
320				body = "(rationale absent)"
321			}
322			return NoChangesOutcome{
323				Satisfied:        false,
324				Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
325				CooldownEligible: false,
326				EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
327				EventBody:        body,
328				Label:            NoChangesLabelUnjustified,
329			}
330		}
331	}
332	
333	func noChangesUnsupportedStatusOutcome(status, reason string) NoChangesOutcome {
334		body := fmt.Sprintf("status: %s rejected: %s", status, reason)
335		return NoChangesOutcome{
336			Satisfied:        false,
337			Action:           NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown,
338			CooldownEligible: false,
339			EventKind:        NoChangesEventUnjustified,
340			EventBody:        body,
341			Reason:           body,
342		}
343	}
344	
345	func noChangesLifecycleEventBody(status, reason, suggestedAction string) string {
346		body := "status=" + status
347		if reason != "" {
348			body += "\nreason=" + reason
349		}
350		if suggestedAction != "" {
351			body += "\nsuggested_action=" + suggestedAction
352		}
353		return body
354	}

20	func TestIntake_ActionableAtomic_ClaimsNormally(t *testing.T) {
21		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
22		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
23	
24		var intakeCalls int32
25		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
26			Store: store,
27			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
28				return ExecuteBeadReport{
29					BeadID:    beadID,
30					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
31					SessionID: "sess-intake",
32					ResultRev: "abc123",
33				}, nil
34			}),
35		}
36	
37		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
38		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
39	
40		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
41			Once: true,
42			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
43				atomic.AddInt32(&intakeCalls, 1)
44				return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
45			},
46		})
47		require.NoError(t, err)
48		require.NotNil(t, result)
49	
50		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeCalls), "intake hook must run once")
51		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "actionable atomic intake must proceed to Claim")
52		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
53		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
54	
55		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
56		require.NoError(t, err)
57		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, got.Status)
58	}

60	func TestIntake_InfrastructureErrorFailsOpen(t *testing.T) {
61		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
62		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
63	
64		var eventSink bytes.Buffer

68				return ExecuteBeadReport{

100		var preflightSeen int32

5:// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
6:// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
7:type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
10:	PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
11:	PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
12:	PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
13:	PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"

1	package agent
2	
3	import "context"
4	
5	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
6	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
7	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
8	
9	const (
10		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
11		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
12		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
13		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
14		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
15	)
16	
17	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
18	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
19	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
20		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
21		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
22		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
23		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
24		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
25	}
26	
27	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
28	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
29	// claim.
30	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
31		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
32		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
33		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
34	}
35	
36	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
37	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
38	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
39	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
40	
41	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
42		switch r.Outcome {
43		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
44			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
45		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
46			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
47			return r.Outcome
48		default:
49			return PreClaimIntakeError
50		}
51	}

76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}
105	
106	// ResolveBeadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore returns the effective lint block
107	// threshold. Defaults to 0, which means warn-only.
108	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveBeadQualityLintBlockThresholdScore() int {
109		if c == nil || c.BeadQuality == nil || c.BeadQuality.Lint == nil || c.BeadQuality.Lint.BlockThresholdScore == nil {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if *c.BeadQuality.Lint.BlockThresholdScore <= 0 {
113			return 0
114		}
115		return *c.BeadQuality.Lint.BlockThresholdScore
116	}

24	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
25	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
26	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
27	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
28	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
29		Log             io.Writer
30		CleanupLog      io.Writer
31		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
32		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
33		CleanupInterval time.Duration
34		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
35		EventSink       io.Writer
36		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
37		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
38		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
39		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
40		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
41		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
42		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
43		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
44		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
45		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
46		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
47		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
48		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
49		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
50		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
51		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
52		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
53		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
54		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
55		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
56		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
57		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
58		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
59		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
60		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
61		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
62		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
63		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
64		Mode         executeloop.Mode
65		IdleInterval time.Duration
66		Once         bool
67		PollInterval time.Duration
68		NoReview     bool
69		LabelFilter  string
70		SessionID    string
71		WorkerID     string
72		ProjectRoot  string
73		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
74		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
75		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
76		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
77		TargetBeadID string
78		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
79		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
80		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
81		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
82		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
83		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
84		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
85	}
86	
87	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
88		mode := r.Mode
89		if mode == "" {
90			if r.Once {
91				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
92			} else {
93				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
94			}
95		}
96		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
97		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
98			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
99		}
100		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
101			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
102		}
103		return mode, idleInterval
104	}

106	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
107	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
108	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
109	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
110	
111	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
112	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
113	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
114	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
115	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
116	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
117	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
118	
119	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
120	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
121	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
122	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
123	// advance the base branch.
124	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
125	
126	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
127	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
128	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
129	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
130	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
131	// worker.
132	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
133	
134	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
135	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
136	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
137	// triage:needs-investigation park.
138	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
139

143	// the worker's result under refs/ddx/iterations/, records checks-blocked

1	# Intake Gate — Pre-Claim Actionability And Complexity Evaluator
2	
3	The intake gate is a pre-Claim phase in `ddx work` that evaluates each
4	candidate bead before an agent worker claims it. Its purpose is to improve beads
5	that can be safely clarified, prevent coarse epics from being dispatched as
6	monolithic work items, and hold ambiguous work for operator decision before an
7	implementer is forced to guess.
8	
9	## When the gate triggers
10	
11	The gate runs for every ready bead before `Store.Claim`. It evaluates the bead's
12	title, description, acceptance criteria, labels, parent, dependencies, spec-id,
13	and prior attempt history to classify it as:
14	
15	- **actionable_atomic** — a single coherent unit of work; proceeds to Claim
16	  normally.
17	- **actionable_but_rewritten** — the gate made a validated replacement rewrite
18	  or other safe, intent-preserving bead update through `ddx bead update`;
19	  proceeds to Claim after the mutation.
20	- **too_large_decomposed** — multiple independent deliverables; the gate files
21	  child beads, records the AC map, and leaves the parent `status=open` with
22	  dependency edges to the children.
23	- **ambiguous_requires_operator** — needs human clarification; the gate moves
24	  the bead to `status=proposed`.
25	
26	Safe rewrites may replace the bead body when the replacement is clearer,
27	execution-ready, and validated against durable anchors. The gate should not
28	append clarifying noise simply to satisfy preservation: the output bead is the
29	next agent's prompt, so prompt fitness is part of correctness. Some replacements
30	expand a vague one-line bead into a standalone task; others compress noisy or
31	stale prose. The original text is retained in readiness evidence with
32	before/after hashes.
33	
34	Validated preservation is based on explicit commitments and durable context:
35	acceptance criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies,
36	named files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence. The gate may replace
37	stale line numbers or chat-shaped prose with current section anchors or file:line
38	evidence. It must not invent product behavior, change scope, choose between
39	conflicting requirements, delete unresolved constraints, or guess a missing
40	governing artifact.
41	
42	The same decomposition policy is also used as a post-attempt safety net. If an
43	implementation attempt returns `no_changes_needs_investigation` or an equivalent
44	structured outcome whose rationale says the bead is too large or cannot be
45	safely split inside the implementation worktree, `ddx work` must run the
46	orchestrator-level splitter on the original bead. That fallback is not operator
47	work unless the splitter cannot produce a lossless AC map or the configured
48	decomposition depth has truly been exhausted at the queue level; those cases
49	move the bead to `status=proposed`.
50	
51	## Bypassing the gate per-bead
52	
53	Add the label `triage:skip` to a bead to bypass the complexity gate entirely:
54	
55	```
56	ddx bead update <id> --labels triage:skip
57	```
58	
59	The bead will proceed directly to Claim on the next dispatch cycle. Use this
60	for beads you have manually reviewed and confirmed are atomic, or for beads
61	that the gate misclassifies.
62	
63	## Decomposed parent state
64	
65	When the gate decomposes a bead:
66	
67	1. Child beads are filed with `parent: <id>` linking back to the epic.
68	2. The parent's status remains `open`; dependency waiting is derived from the
69	   new child dependency edges, not `status=blocked`.
70	3. The parent receives a `kind:triage-decomposed` event whose JSON body lists
71	   the `child_ids`, the splitter's `rationale`, and an `ac_map`.
72	4. Dependency edges are added: the parent depends on all children, ensuring it
73	   cannot be dispatched again until children close (should it be re-opened).
74	
75	The `ac_map` is load-bearing. Every parent acceptance criterion must map to at
76	least one child acceptance criterion or be explicitly marked
77	`operator_required` or `non_scope` with a rationale. A split that drops an AC is
78	invalid and moves the parent to `status=proposed` instead of dispatching
79	children as if the work were complete.
80	
81	Children re-enter the triage gate on their next dispatch cycle. If a child is
82	itself decomposable, the gate will split it further — up to the depth cap.
83	
84	## Recursion depth cap
85	
86	Default depth cap: **3** levels. Configurable in `.ddx/config.yaml` at
87	`agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`:
88	
89	```yaml
90	agent:
91	  triage:
92	    max_decomposition_depth: 3
93	```
94	
95	When a bead at the queue-level depth cap is evaluated, the gate:
96	
97	1. Appends a `kind:triage-overflow` event.
98	2. Sets `status=proposed` and records the depth overflow in triage evidence.
99	3. Does **not** invoke the classifier or splitter.
100	4. Does **not** dispatch the bead.
101	
102	Implementation-attempt depth is not the same as queue-level decomposition depth.
103	If a worker reports that it cannot split because the attempt prompt forbids
104	another child layer, the orchestrator still owns the next split decision unless
105	the bead itself has reached `agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth`.
106	
107	The operator must manually split the bead or add `triage:skip` to bypass only
108	after queue-level overflow or lossy/ambiguous decomposition is recorded.
109	
110	## AC-coverage metric
111	
112	The `bead-split` prompt is evaluated against a held-out corpus using both:
113	
114	- hard AC traceability: every parent AC maps to child ACs, `operator_required`, or
115	  `non_scope`;
116	- string-overlap metric: the fraction of unique AC tokens (alphanumeric tokens
117	  ≥ 3 chars) from the parent's acceptance criteria that appear in the combined
118	  acceptance criteria of the child specs.
119	
120	A rate ≥ 90% is required for the heuristic metric, but overlap alone is not
121	acceptance. The hard AC map is the gate that prevents lossy decomposition.
122	
123	## Regenerating the historical corpus
124	
125	The frozen eval slice lives in `library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl`. To
126	regenerate from live bead history:
127	
128	```bash
129	go run ./scripts/triage/harvest-corpus.go \
130	  --output library/prompts/triage/eval-corpus.jsonl
131	```
132	
133	The harvest script reads `.ddx/beads.jsonl` from this repository and includes
134	`../agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl` when that checkout is present. It produces a
135	deterministic held-out eval slice. Running it with the same inputs always
136	produces the same corpus file.
137	
138	## Ownership boundary
139	
140	DDx owns orchestration, tracking, work sizing, child bead creation, parent
141	dependencies, and retry decisions. The agent owns provider/model/harness
142	routing and execution. The triage gate must not choose or rank concrete
143	providers, models, or harnesses; if an operator supplied those values, DDx
144	passes them through as opaque constraints to the agent runtime.
145	
146	Decomposition is a high-judgment routing decision. The splitter must run through
147	the normal `ddx work` path with Fizeau's `smart` model-ref and a strong
148	`MinPower` floor, defaulting to the smart/top-power tier floor when the project
149	has no explicit splitter override. DDx still does not pick a concrete model: it
150	only requests the abstract smart ref, raises the power floor, and lets the agent
151	route within any operator-supplied passthrough constraints. If no available
152	route satisfies that strong floor, DDx records readiness as unavailable
153	(`readiness_error` / `intake_error`) instead of marking the bead as ambiguous or
154	moving it to `status=proposed`. DDx must not silently fall back to weak
155	decomposition; the worker continues, skips, or stops according to the configured
156	readiness-failure mode.
157	
158	## Disabling the gate
159	
160	The gate is disabled when `ExecuteBeadWorker.ComplexityGate == nil`. This is
161	intentionally non-silent: the worker emits a one-time warning per boot:
162	
163	```
164	warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
165	```
166	
167	The gate should only be disabled in test fixtures or during development. In
168	production, always wire a `ComplexityGate` via `NewComplexityGate`.
169	
170	## Tracker events
171	
172	| Event kind              | When                                       | Body                          |
173	|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
174	| `triage-rewritten`      | Safe bead replacement/update applied       | `{fields, rationale, before, after}` |
175	| `triage-decomposed`     | Parent split into children                 | `{child_ids, rationale, ac_map}` |
176	| `triage-overflow`       | Bead at depth cap, blocked                 | `{depth, max}`                |
177	| `triage-ambiguous`      | Gate returned ambiguous classification     | `{confidence, reasoning}`     |
178	| `triage.gate_disabled`  | Loop boot with nil gate (loop event log)   | `{bead_id}`                   |
179	| `triage.skipped`        | Gate returned shouldClaim=false            | `{bead_id}`                   |
180	| `triage.error`          | Gate returned a non-nil error              | `{bead_id, reason}`           |
181

15	func TestAttempt_WrapsLegacyExecutor(t *testing.T) {
16		out, err := Attempt(context.Background(), nil, "ddx-test", AttemptOpts{
17			Executor: ExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (Report, error) {
18				return Report{BeadID: beadID, Status: StatusSuccess, ResultRev: "abc123"}, nil
19			}),
20		})
21		require.NoError(t, err)
22	
23		assert.Equal(t, OutcomeReported, out.Disposition)
24		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-test", out.Report.BeadID)
25		assert.Equal(t, StatusSuccess, out.Report.Status)
26		assert.Equal(t, "abc123", out.Report.ResultRev)
27	}

29	func TestAttempt_NoChangesRejectsLegacyNeedsInvestigationStatus(t *testing.T) {
30		store := &attemptStore{}
31	
32		out, err := Attempt(context.Background(), store, "ddx-test", AttemptOpts{
33			Store: store,
34			Executor: ExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (Report, error) {
35				return Report{
36					BeadID:             beadID,
37					Status:             StatusNoChanges,
38					NoChangesRationale: "status: needs_investigation\nreason: provider quota unknown",
39				}, nil
40			}),
41		})
42		require.NoError(t, err)
43	
44		assert.Equal(t, OutcomeReported, out.Disposition)
45		assert.Equal(t, StatusNoChanges, out.Report.Status)
46		require.NotNil(t, out.NoChanges)
47		assert.False(t, out.NoChanges.Satisfied)
48		assert.Equal(t, NoChangesActionBadAttemptNoCooldown, out.NoChanges.Action)
49		assert.False(t, out.NoChanges.CooldownEligible)
50		assert.Equal(t, NoChangesEventLegacyStatusRejected, out.NoChanges.EventKind)
51		assert.Empty(t, out.NoChanges.Label)
52		assert.Contains(t, out.NoChanges.EventBody, "status: needs_investigation is no longer accepted")
53		assert.Contains(t, out.NoChanges.EventBody, "ddx bead migrate --lifecycle")
54		assert.Contains(t, out.NoChanges.EventBody, "provider quota unknown")
55		assert.Equal(t, 1, store.noChangesCountCalls)
56	}

75			},
76		})

79		assert.Equal(t, OutcomeReported, out.Disposition)

81		require.NotNil(t, out.NoChanges)

83		assert.Equal(t, NoChangesActionCloseAlreadySatisfied, out.NoChanges.Action)
