24	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
25	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
26	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
27	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
28	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
29		Log             io.Writer
30		CleanupLog      io.Writer
31		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
32		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
33		CleanupInterval time.Duration
34		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
35		EventSink       io.Writer
36		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
37		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
38		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
39		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
40		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
41		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
42		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
43		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
44		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
45		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
46		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
47		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
48		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
49		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
50		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
51		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
52		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
53		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
54		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
55		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
56		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
57		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
58		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
59		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
60		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
61		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
62		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
63		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
64		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
65		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
66		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
67		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
68		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
69		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
70		Mode         executeloop.Mode
71		IdleInterval time.Duration
72		Once         bool
73		PollInterval time.Duration
74		NoReview     bool
75		LabelFilter  string
76		SessionID    string
77		WorkerID     string
78		ProjectRoot  string
79		// TargetBeadID, when non-empty, restricts nextCandidate to only return the
80		// named bead from the execution-ready queue. Used by `ddx try <bead-id>`
81		// to dispatch a single specific bead through the same claim → executor →
82		// land path the queue drain uses. When empty, the picker behaves normally.
83		TargetBeadID string
84		// WakeCh, when non-nil, signals the idle-poll sleep to return immediately
85		// so the loop re-scans the queue. Used by the operator-prompt approve /
86		// auto-approve mutations (Story 15) to avoid an idle-interval-sized delay
87		// before a freshly-approved bead is claimed. Implementations must send
88		// non-blocking (server-side helpers do); the loop only waits for a
89		// receive on WakeCh during the idle sleep, never elsewhere.
90		WakeCh <-chan struct{}
91	}
92	
93	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
94		mode := r.Mode
95		if mode == "" {
96			if r.Once {
97				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
98			} else {
99				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
100			}

119					BeadID:    beadID,
120					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
121					SessionID: "sess-intake-order",
122					ResultRev: "fedcba",
123				}, nil
124			}),
125		}
126	
127		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker", Harness: "claude", Model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"}
128		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
129	
130		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
131			Once: true,
132			RoutePreflight: func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
133				atomic.StoreInt32(&preflightSeen, 1)
134				return nil
135			},
136			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
137				if atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen) == 0 {
138					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after route preflight")
139				}
140				if atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls) == 0 {
141					t.Fatal("PreClaimIntakeHook must run after Claim")
142				}
143				atomic.StoreInt32(&intakeSeen, 1)
144				return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
145			},
146		})
147		require.NoError(t, err)
148		require.NotNil(t, result)
149	
150		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightSeen), "route preflight must run")
151		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&intakeSeen), "intake hook must run")
152		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "Claim must run before intake")
153	}

246	func TestIntake_LogsStartBeforeHookReturns(t *testing.T) {
247		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
248		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
249	
250		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
251			Store: store,
252			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
253				return ExecuteBeadReport{
254					BeadID:    beadID,
255					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
256					SessionID: "sess-intake-start",
257					ResultRev: "abc789",
258				}, nil
259			}),
260		}
261	
262		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
263		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
264	
265		started := make(chan struct{})
266		release := make(chan struct{})
267		done := make(chan error, 1)
268		var log bytes.Buffer
269		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
270	
271		go func() {
272			_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
273				Once:      true,
274				Log:       &log,
275				EventSink: &eventSink,
276				PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
277					close(started)
278					<-release
279					return PreClaimIntakeResult{Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic}, nil
280				},
281			})
282			done <- err
283		}()
284	
285		select {
286		case <-started:
287		case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
288			t.Fatal("intake hook did not start")
289		}
290		assert.Contains(t, log.String(), "readiness check: starting")
291		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.start")
292	
293		close(release)
294		require.NoError(t, <-done)
295		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls))
296	}

378	func TestIntake_ActionableButRewritten_UpdatesAfterClaim(t *testing.T) {
379		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
380		require.NoError(t, inner.Update(candidate.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
381			b.Description = "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n\nNON-SCOPE\nchange no product semantics\n"
382			b.Acceptance = "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command"
383		}))
384	
385		var claimSeenBeforeRewrite int32
386		store := &claimCountingStore{
387			Store: inner,
388			beforeClaim: func() {
389				// Claim now happens BEFORE intake, so the description must NOT
390				// be rewritten yet when beforeClaim is called.
391				got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
392				require.NoError(t, err)
393				if strings.Contains(got.Description, "Add an explicit validation step.") {
394					t.Fatalf("description must NOT be rewritten before Claim; got %q", got.Description)
395				}
396				atomic.StoreInt32(&claimSeenBeforeRewrite, 1)
397			},
398		}
399	
400		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
401		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
402			Store: store,
403			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
404				return ExecuteBeadReport{
405					BeadID:    beadID,
406					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
407					SessionID: "sess-intake-rewrite",
408					ResultRev: "abc123",
409				}, nil
410			}),
411		}
412	
413		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
414		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
415	
416		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
417			Once:      true,
418			EventSink: &eventSink,
419			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
420				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
421					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
422					Detail:  "tightened scope and verification",
423					Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
424						Description:   "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n\nNON-SCOPE\nchange no product semantics\n\nAdd an explicit validation step.",
425						Acceptance:    "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command\n3. lefthook run pre-commit",
426						ChangedFields: []string{"description", "acceptance"},
427					},
428				}, nil
429			},
430		})
431		require.NoError(t, err)
432		require.NotNil(t, result)
433	
434		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "rewritten intake must still proceed to Claim")
435		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&claimSeenBeforeRewrite), "claim must happen before rewrite")
436		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
437		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
438	
439		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
440		require.NoError(t, err)
441		assert.Contains(t, got.Description, "Add an explicit validation step.")
442		assert.Contains(t, got.Acceptance, "lefthook run pre-commit")
443	
444		events, err := inner.Events(candidate.ID)
445		require.NoError(t, err)
446		var found bool
447		for _, ev := range events {
448			if ev.Kind != "intake-rewritten" {
449				continue
450			}
451			found = true
452			assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "description")
453			assert.Contains(t, ev.Summary, "acceptance")
454			var body map[string]any
455			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
456			assert.Equal(t, "tightened scope and verification", body["rationale"])
457			assert.Contains(t, body, "before")
458			assert.Contains(t, body, "after")
459			assert.NotContains(t, ev.Body, "MODE: intake")
460			assert.NotContains(t, ev.Body, "legacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE")
461		}
462		require.True(t, found, "rewritten intake must record an intake-rewritten event")
463	}

465	func TestIntake_UnsafeRewriteBlocksForHuman(t *testing.T) {
466		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
467		require.NoError(t, inner.Update(candidate.ID, func(b *bead.Bead) {
468			b.Description = "PROBLEM\nlegacy intake is vague\n\nROOT CAUSE\nmissing constraints\n\nPROPOSED FIX\nrewrite the bead\n"
469			b.Acceptance = "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command"
470		}))
471	
472		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
473		var eventSink bytes.Buffer
474		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
475			Store: store,
476			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
477				t.Fatal("executor must not run for unsafe rewrites")
478				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
479			}),
480		}
481	
482		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
483		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
484	
485		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
486			Once:      true,
487			EventSink: &eventSink,
488			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
489				return PreClaimIntakeResult{
490					Outcome: PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten,
491					Detail:  "attempted to drop acceptance criteria",
492					Rewrite: PreClaimIntakeRewrite{
493						Description:   "PROBLEM\ninvented product semantics\n",
494						Acceptance:    "1. preserve the original intent",
495						ChangedFields: []string{"description", "acceptance"},
496					},
497				}, nil
498			},
499		})
500		require.NoError(t, err)
501		require.NotNil(t, result)
502	
503		// Claim now happens before intake; for unsafe rewrites the bead is
504		// claimed then immediately unclaimed, so claimCalls == 1 but execution
505		// does not proceed and the bead description/acceptance must be unchanged.
506		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "claim runs before intake check")
507		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "pre_claim_intake.blocked")
508		assert.Contains(t, eventSink.String(), "operator_required")
509		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
510	
511		got, err := inner.Get(candidate.ID)
512		require.NoError(t, err)
513		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusProposed, got.Status, "rejected rewrite must move bead to operator attention")
514		assert.NotContains(t, got.Description, "invented product semantics", "original description must be preserved")
515		assert.Equal(t, "1. preserve the original intent\n2. name the verification command", got.Acceptance)
516		assert.NotContains(t, got.Labels, bead.LabelNeedsHuman, "operator parking must be status-owned, not needs_human label-owned")
517		assert.Contains(t, bead.GetNeedsHumanMeta(*got).Reason, "acceptance criteria")
518	
519		events, err := inner.Events(candidate.ID)
520		require.NoError(t, err)
521		var foundIntakeBlocked bool
522		for _, ev := range events {
523			if ev.Kind != "intake.blocked" {
524				continue
525			}
526			foundIntakeBlocked = true
527			var body map[string]any
528			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
529			assert.Equal(t, "operator_required", body["intake_outcome"])
530		}
531		assert.True(t, foundIntakeBlocked, "rejected rewrite must append intake.blocked event to bead")
532	}

632			var body map[string]any
633			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.Body), &body))
634			assert.Equal(t, "operator_required", body["intake_outcome"])
635			assert.Contains(t, fmt.Sprintf("%v", body["detail"]), "drops commitment")
636		}
637		assert.True(t, foundBlocked, "description preservation failure must append intake.blocked event")
638		assert.Contains(t, logBuf.String(), "parking")
639	}
640	
641	// TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix asserts that when the intake
642	// hook returns an error already prefixed with "pre-claim intake:", the operator
643	// log renders a single user-facing prefix and never contains the doubled form
644	// "pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake:".
645	func TestReadinessHookEmptyOutput_DeduplicatesPrefix(t *testing.T) {
646		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
647		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
648	
649		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
650			Store: store,
651			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
652				return ExecuteBeadReport{
653					BeadID:    beadID,
654					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
655					SessionID: "sess-dedup",
656					ResultRev: "abc000",
657				}, nil
658			}),
659		}
660	
661		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
662		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
663	
664		var log bytes.Buffer
665		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
666			Once: true,
667			Log:  &log,
668			PreClaimIntakeHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error) {
669				// Simulate the exact error format returned by intakeResultPayload.
670				return PreClaimIntakeResult{}, errors.New("pre-claim intake: empty output")
671			},
672		})
673		require.NoError(t, err)
674		require.NotNil(t, result)
675	
676		logOut := log.String()
677		assert.NotContains(t, logOut, "pre-claim intake: pre-claim intake:", "doubled prefix must not appear")
678		assert.Contains(t, logOut, "readiness check unavailable: empty output (continuing)")
679		// Execution must still proceed (fail-open semantics).
680		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
681	}
682	
683	// TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable asserts that a missing-harness
684	// lint hook failure renders as an actionable readiness-check warning in the
685	// operator log rather than exposing the raw "lint hook: missing-harness" error.
686	func TestReadinessUnavailableOutputIsActionable(t *testing.T) {
687		inner, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
688		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
689	
690		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
691			Store: store,
692			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
693				return ExecuteBeadReport{
694					BeadID:    beadID,
695					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
696					SessionID: "sess-harness-warn",
697					ResultRev: "def000",
698				}, nil
699			}),
700		}
701	
702		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
703		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
704	
705		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
706		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
707			Once: true,
708			Log:  &logBuf,
709			PreDispatchLintHook: func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error) {
710				return LintResult{}, ErrLintHookMissingHarness
711			},
712		})
713		require.NoError(t, err)
714		require.NotNil(t, result)
715	
716		logOut := logBuf.String()
717		assert.NotContains(t, logOut, "lint hook: missing-harness", "raw lint hook error must not appear in operator log")
718		assert.NotContains(t, logOut, "pre-dispatch lint:", "pre-dispatch lint prefix must not appear in operator log")
719		assert.Contains(t, logOut, "readiness check unavailable: no harness configured; continuing")
720		// Fail-open: execution must proceed.
721		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
722	}
723

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.

14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"

17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)

20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`

33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.

37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`

41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {

58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false

79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and

323	// CapLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
324	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
325	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
326	func CapLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
327		if d <= 0 {

330		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
331			return MaxLoopCooldown
332		}

61	// when RoutePreflight returns the upstream typed-incompatibility error
62	// (agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible), the loop must fail during startup
63	// before any bead is claimed or executed.
64	func TestRoutingPreflightRejectionExitsLoopWithoutClaim(t *testing.T) {
65		inner, candidate, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
66		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
67	
68		var execCount int32
69		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
70			Store: store,
71			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
72				atomic.AddInt32(&execCount, 1)
73				return ExecuteBeadReport{BeadID: beadID, Status: ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess}, nil
74			}),
75		}
76	
77		// Fabricate a typed-incompatibility error via the upstream test seam:
78		// the preflight callback returns the same error type that upstream
79		// ResolveRoute returns when a harness allow-list rejects the model.
80		rejected := agentlib.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible{
81			Harness:         "claude",
82			Model:           "gpt-5",
83			SupportedModels: []string{"claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6"},
84		}
85		var preflightCalls int32
86		preflight := func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
87			atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
88			return rejected
89		}
90	
91		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
92			Assignee: "worker",
93			Harness:  "claude",
94			Model:    "gpt-5",
95		}
96		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
97	
98		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
99			Once:           true,
100			RoutePreflight: preflight,
101		})
102		require.NoError(t, err)
103		require.NotNil(t, result)
104	
105		// AC#1: preflight ran exactly once during startup.
106		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must be invoked")
107	
108		// AC#2: Claim was NEVER called on the store.
109		assert.Equal(t, int32(0), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls),
110			"beadStore.Claim must not be called when preflight rejects")

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(agent): auto-decompose too-large beads at intake and post-attempt [ddx-008d288f]

Add queue-level auto-decomposition path for beads flagged as too large:
- PreClaimIntake: when outcome is too_large_decomposed, create child beads, wire
  parent→child dep edges, emit triage-decomposed event; validate ACMap completeness
  before creating children (lossy ACMap parks to proposed, operator required)
- Depth cap check before intake hook: if bead depth >= agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth
  emit triage-overflow event, add needs-human-decomposition label, park to proposed
- Post-attempt: when no_changes rationale contains orchestrator_action: decompose,
  invoke PostAttemptDecompositionHook using queue-level depth cap; valid complete
  decomposition creates children and wires deps; lossy or nil hook result parks operator
- Update instrStep0SizeCheck to clarify implementation-level depth cap (2) is advisory;
  orchestrator uses agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth (default 3)
- Add orchestrator_action: decompose documentation to instrNoChangesContract
- Update byte ceilings (6400/6200) and word floor (0.78) for AC8-required additions
- MaxDecompositionDepth wired through config.ResolvedConfig and TestLoopConfigOpts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
